title: Main Editors
text: Main Editors Each main development object is opened in it own editor. You can have multiple objects open at one time and easily switch between them using the editor tabs. The type of object (Project, Entity, Component, Library, and so on) determines the tools provided by the editor. An editor has at least one worksheet , which groups the tools required for a specific activity. For example, the Component Editor has worksheets for defining the component structure, writing script, and designing the component layout. The tools provided in each worksheet depend on the main development object, but typically include the following: Resource Browser on the left, for browsing and inserting templates, development objects, and code snippets. Worksheet editor in the middle, for performing the primary task of the worksheet, such as defining the object contents and structure, or editing script. Properties Inspector on the right, for viewing and editing the properties of the object selected in the worksheet editor. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
